title: A water circulation system for aquatic animals and hatchery
abstract: The present invention discloses a water circulation system for breeding aquatic animals and hatchery comprises an aquaculture tank (10) having means (12, 13) for removing feces polluted water from the tank (10); means (20, 30) for filtering feces particles from the polluted water; means (40) for skimming organic compounds from the filtered water; means (50) for reducing ammonia level of the skimmed water; means (60) for sanitizing and oxidizing the ammonia reduced water; means (70) for exchanging oxygen into sanitised and oxidized water; and means for directing the processed water back to the tank to form a water circulation; characterised in that the means of the system are linked synergistically to produce reusable water to the tank (10) for the aquatic animals and hatchery without needs of changing fresh water into the tank.
claims:
Claims 1. A water circulation system for breeding aquatic animals and hatchery comprising: an aquaculture tank (10) having means (12, 13) for removing feces polluted water from the tank (10); means (20, 30) for filtering feces particles from the polluted water; means (40) for skimming organic compounds from the filtered water; means (50) for reducing ammonia level of the skimmed water; means (60) for sanitizing and oxidizing the ammonia reduced water; means (70) for exchanging oxygen into sanitised and oxidized water; and means for directing the processed water back to the tank to form a water circulation; characterised in that the means of the system are linked synergistically to produce reusable water to the tank (10) for the aquatic animals and hatchery without needs of changing fresh water into the tank. 2. The water circulation system of claim 1, further comprising means (80) for pumping the water circulation. 3. The water circulation system of claim 1, wherein the aquaculture tank (10) comprises a generally cylindrical vessel with an open-end top section, a generally frusto-conical bottom section, and a plurality of equally spaced supports (11) beneath the bottom section. 4. The water circulation system of claim 3, wherein the bottom section of the aquaculture tank (10) has at least one primary opening (12) linked to the feces particles filtering means (20, 30) via at least one water pipe with a valve disposed therebetween. 5. The water circulation system of claim 3, wherein the aquaculture tank (10) further 

 comprises at least one secondary opening (13) adjacent its top section for directing overflowing water out from the tank (10), and the secondary opening (13) is linked to the feces particles filtering means (20, 30) via at least one water pipe with a valve disposed therebetween. 6. The water circulation system of claim 3, wherein the supports (11) of the aquaculture tank (10) is radially spaced apart, each having a narrowed inner section and a top surface downwardly inclined towards the narrowed inner section. 7. The water circulation system of claim 1, wherein the filtering feces means (20, 30) comprises a primary filtering unit (20) having a hollow body having an open end tube disposed therewithin at or adjacent its centre axis and extending upwardly from the bottom of the body to a height not higher than the height of the body so that water in the body can overflow into the tube via the open end; an inlet linked to the bottom section of the body for directing water from the aquaculture tank (20) into the body; and an outlet linked to the bottom section of the tube for directing the water within the tube to the secondary filtering unit (30). 8. The water circulation system of claim 1, wherein the filtering feces means (20, 30) comprises a secondary filtering unit (30) having a hollow body having two spaced layers of mesh screens that regionally separate the body into a bottom section, a top section and a middle section sandwiched therebetween, a plurality of floatable pellets or resins are disposed in the middle section; an inlet linked to the bottom section for directing water from the primary filtering unit (20) to the body; an outlet linked to the top section for directing water to the means (40) for skimming organic compounds and means for stirring the floatable pellets or resins. 9. The water circulation system of claim 1, wherein the means (40) for skimming organic compounds is a skimmer which includes a hollow body having an open ended 

 tube extended upwardly from the bottom of the body and the tube has at least one opening at its bottom section; a first inlet linked to top section of the tube for directing water from the filtering feces means (20, 30) into the tube; a second inlet disposed beneath the first inlet and linked to the tube for introducing bubbles into the tube via a venturi mechanism; a detachable collector disposed at the top opening of the tube for collecting organic forms; and an outlet linked to the top section of the body for directing the water to the means (50) for reducing ammonia level. 10. The water circulation system of claim 1, wherein the means (50) for reducing ammonia level is a bio reactor which includes a hollow body having a tube extended upwardly from the bottom of the body and it has at least one opening at its bottom section; a mesh screen separated the body into an upper section and a lower section, in which the lower section is filed with a plurality of bio balls; an inlet linked to the top section of the tube for directing water from the means (40) for skimming organic compounds into the tube; and an outlet linked to the upper section of the body for directing the water to the means (60) for sanitizing and oxidizing. 11. The water circulation system of claim 1, wherein the sanitizing and oxidizing means (60) includes a hollow body having a tube extended upwardly from the bottom of the body and it has at least one opening at its bottom section; a first inlet linked to the top section of the tube for directing the water from the means (50) for reducing ammonia level into the tube, a second inlet for introducing ozonated water into the tube via a venturi mechanism which coupled with an ozone generator; an outlet linked to the top section of the body for directing the water to the means (70) for exchanghing oxygen. 12. The water circulation system of claim 1, wherein the exchanging oxygen means (70) is a trickling filter which includes a hollow body having an open ended tube extended upwardly from the bottom of the body and it has at least one opening at its 

 bottom section; an inlet linked to the top section of the tube for directing the water from the sanitizing and oxidizing means (60) into the tube; an outlet linked to the top section of the body for directing the water back to the tank (10). 13. The water circulation system of claim 11 or claim 12, wherein the sanitizing and oxidizing means (60) or the exchanging oxygen means (70) further includes means for emitting ultraviolet radiation. 14. The water circulation system of claim 13, wherein the means for emitting ultraviolet radiation is a plurality of ultraviolet light tubes arranged in a way such that they surround their respective tube.
